name: Nami
playstyleSummary: Nami is a support mage who heals and speeds up her allies while landing a long-range stun to start fights for her team. She feels like a facilitator - much of her power comes from empowering an ally's engage or peeling for a teammate under pressure, rather than dealing damage herself.
strengths: Aqua Prison is a strong long-range stun for starting fights or saving allies
Ebb and Flow gives consistent healing and poke from a single cast
Tidal Wave is a powerful teamfight engage that knocks up and slows a wide area
Surging Tides adds useful move speed to any ally she touches with an ability
weaknesses: Aqua Prison has a long cooldown and must land as a skillshot to be effective
Low base damage and toughness compared to other champions
Positioning Tidal Wave and Ebb and Flow well takes practice
Relies on teammates to follow up on the fights she starts

abilities.name: Surging Tides
abilities.howToUseIt: Landing abilities on allies gives them bonus move speed, so time your heals and stun right before an ally engages, chases, or needs to retreat.

abilities.name: Aqua Prison
abilities.howToUseIt: This is your main engage and peel tool - a skillshot that stuns everyone it lands on, so use it to start fights or to save an ally being chased.

abilities.name: Ebb and Flow
abilities.howToUseIt: Cast this toward an ally to heal them or toward an enemy to poke them - aim it so it bounces between the two to get both effects from one cast.

abilities.name: Tidecaller's Blessing
abilities.howToUseIt: Empower an ally who's about to trade or all-in - their next attacks and spells deal bonus damage and slow, so cast it right before a fight rather than mid-fight.

abilities.name: Tidal Wave
abilities.howToUseIt: Send this out early since it travels slowly - use it to knock up and slow enemies from far away, or to double the move speed boost allies get from Surging Tides.

earlyGamePlan: Stick close to your ADC and look for chances to land Aqua Prison on an overextended enemy to set up a kill. Use Ebb and Flow to heal your lane partner during trades while also poking the enemy, since it hits both in one cast.
quickTips: Aqua Prison has a long cooldown, so don't waste it on a low-value target - wait for a real engage opportunity.
Aim Ebb and Flow so it bounces between your ally and an enemy to get healing and poke from a single cast.
Send Tidal Wave out early since it travels slowly across the map - fights won't wait for it.
Use Tidecaller's Blessing on an ally right before they commit to a trade, not after.
Since you have no escape, stay behind minions and let your ADC or jungler create engage opportunities for you.
whatToExpect: Nami will look to land Aqua Prison on you from range to set up a kill for her team, and can peel for her allies by healing and speeding them up with Ebb and Flow. Watch for the long, slow-moving Tidal Wave in teamfights - it telegraphs where it's going, so you can usually walk out of its path if you're paying attention.

glossary.definition: Starting a fight, usually by committing crowd control or a dash onto an enemy.
Protecting an ally, often a carry, by slowing, stunning, or blocking enemies attacking them.
An ability that must be aimed manually and can be dodged, rather than one that automatically hits its target.
Chip damage traded from range to wear enemies down before a real fight.
Exchanging damage with an opponent, usually in lane.
Committing all your damage and cooldowns to try to kill an enemy right now.
An ability used to quickly get away from danger.
Pushed too far forward, away from allies, turret protection, or vision.
